perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding clause 3.1 of this Agreement, the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information : a) to its and its Affiliate’s Representatives , provided always that the Receiving Party procures that such Representatives shall comply with the confidentiality obligations as set out in clause 3 of this Agreement , and the Receiving P arty agrees to be liable for the actions or omissions of such Representatives in relation to the Confidential Information as if they were the actions or omissions of the Receiving Party
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding clause 3.1 of this Agreement, the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information : a) to its Representatives, without needing to procure compliance with confidentiality obligations; the Receiving Party is not liable for actions/omissions of Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the conditions under which Confidential Information can be disclosed to Representatives. The original requires the Receiving Party to ensure the Representatives comply with confidentiality obligations and holds the Receiving Party liable for their actions. The changed text removes both these requirements. This directly contradicts the original confidentiality obligations outlined in clause 3 and affects liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Disclosing Party disclosing Confidential Information to the Receiving Party, the Receiv ing Party undertakes that it shall: a) keep the Confidential Inf ormation secret and confidential, using at least the same degree of care as its uses to protect its own confidential information but no less than a reasonable degree of care ; b) not use or exploit t he Confidential Information in any way except for the Purpose; c) not disclose or make available any Confidential Information in whole or in part to any person except as expressly permitted by and in accordance with this Agreement ; and d) to establish and maint ain adequate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access or use.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Receiving Party does not need to procure compliance with confidentiality obligations from its representatives and is not liable for their actions. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the Receiving Party to keep the Confidential Information secret and confidential and not disclose it to any person except as expressly permitted, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction because it creates inconsistent obligations concerning confidentiality. Furthermore, it aligns with Omissions - In Text Contradiction because it removes critical elements, like the Receiving Party's responsibility for the representatives.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Disclosing Party ’s written request , the Receiving Party shall (as requested by the Disclosing Party) either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by the Receiving Party) all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party including all copies, reproductions, notes , extracts and summaries which include, reflect, incorporate or otherwise contain the Disclosing Party’s Confidenti al Infor mation whether in tangible form or otherwise, such as electronic mail or computer files .
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party may, at its sole discretion, return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party is not obligated to confirm any destruction in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The 'Return or Destruction of Confidential Information' clause has been modified. The original mandates the Receiving Party to return or destroy the information upon request. The modification gives the Receiving Party the discretion to choose whether to comply and removes the requirement to confirm destruction in writing. This introduces ambiguity as it weakens the Disclosing Party's control over their Confidential Information, directly conflicting with the original intention of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Disclosing Party disclosing Confidential Information to the Receiving Party, the Receiv ing Party undertakes that it shall: a) keep the Confidential Inf ormation secret and confidential, using at least the same degree of care as its uses to protect its own confidential information but no less than a reasonable degree of care ; b) not use or exploit t he Confidential Information in any way except for the Purpose; c) not disclose or make available any Confidential Information in whole or in part to any person except as expressly permitted by and in accordance with this Agreement ; and d) to establish and maint ain adequate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access or use.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the Receiving Party's obligation regarding confidential information. The original text mandated return or destruction upon the Disclosing Party's request, whereas the changed text grants the Receiving Party sole discretion, thus contradicting the contradicted text, which states the Receiving Party 'undertakes that it shall: a) keep the Confidential Information secret and confidential'. The changed text creates a direct conflict because it allows the Receiving Party to potentially not return or destroy confidential information, thereby violating the undertaking to keep it confidential. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' since obligations are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: The rights and obligations contained in this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect for [two (2) ] years after expiration of the Term or termination of this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: The rights and obligations contained in this Agreement shall cease immediately upon expiration of the Term or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The clause concerning the survival of confidentiality obligations after the agreement's term is altered. Originally, these obligations lasted for two years after termination. The modification stipulates that they cease immediately upon termination. This creates a direct in-text contradiction regarding the lifespan of confidentiality protections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The provisions of this Agreement shall continue to apply to any documents and materials retained by the Receiving Party pursuant to clause 5.2 of this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that rights and obligations would continue for two years after termination, while the changed text states they cease immediately. The contradicted text states that the provisions of the agreement apply to retained documents pursuant to clause 5.2. If the rights and obligations cease immediately as per the changed text, this contradicts the contradicted text because the provisions of the agreement (rights and obligations) would no longer apply to retained documents even though clause 5.2 expects them to, thus a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Any notice given to a Party under or in connection with this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by hand , by pre -paid first class post or by a reputable next -day courier service at its registered office or principal place of business or sent by email to the address specified by the Party in writing . Any notice shall be deemed to have been received : if delivered by hand, at the time the notice is delivered at the proper address ; if sent by pre -paid first class post, on the second business day after posting ; if by next -day courier service, at the time recorded by the courier; or if by email, at the time of transmission. This clause does not apply to the service of any proceedings or other documents in any legal action or, where applicable, any arbitration or other method of dispute resolution.
perturbation.changed_text: Any notice given to a Party under or in connection with this Agreement may be given orally, and shall be deemed received immediately. Written notice remains an option, delivered by hand, with receipt upon delivery.
perturbation.explanation: The 'Notice' clause is significantly changed. The original mandates written notice through specific methods (hand delivery, post, courier, or email) and defines when notice is considered received. The modification allows oral notice, deemed received immediately, creating an in-text contradiction with other clauses that may require written notification, such as requests for returning confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the Disclosing Party ’s written request , the Receiving Party shall (as requested by the Disclosing Party) either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by the Receiving Party) all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party including all copies, reproductions, notes , extracts and summaries which include, reflect, incorporate or otherwise contain the Disclosing Party’s Confidenti al Infor mation whether in tangible form or otherwise, such as electronic mail or computer files .
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that notices must be in writing. The changed text allows for oral notices, deemed received immediately. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which requires a 'written request' for the return or destruction of Confidential Information. Allowing oral notice makes the written request requirement unenforceable, because there isn't a way to be certain when a written request was made.
